description_long: This object is a cylindrical electronic component with a reddish-brown ceramic or composite body and two metallic wire leads extending from either end. The surface of the cylinder shows signs of wear, with some dark smudges and faded markings, possibly indicating its resistance value or manufacturer. The leads are bent at sharp angles, suggesting it is designed to be mounted through holes on a circuit board. The body is solid and robust, typical of power resistors used in electrical circuits to dissipate heat. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with no decorative elements, and the object is small enough to be easily handled or installed by hand. Its construction suggests it is intended for use in urban electrical infrastructure, such as street lighting, control panels, or utility boxes.
description: A cylindrical reddish-brown electronic resistor with two bent metal leads and faded surface markings, showing signs of wear.
description_view_0: The cylindrical body and both bent metal leads are clearly visible, with faded black markings on the surface.
description_view_1: The resistor is seen from an angle emphasizing the right lead and the worn, marked surface.
description_view_2: The opposite side of the cylinder is visible, showing a mostly unmarked, worn surface and both leads.
description_view_3: The view highlights the left lead and the cleaner side of the resistor body, with less visible marking.
